converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Michael G. Shively papers
Shively, Michael G., 1947-
7671
3.4 cubic feet. 3.4 cubic feet.
1976-1995. Manuscripts, printed materials, working files. Michael Shively's personal papers, including files from his work as Associate Director of the Center for Homosexual, Education, Evaluation, and Research (CHEER) at San Francisco State University, 1975-1980; his work for Liberation Publications Inc., which published the ADVOCATE, 1980-90; and his work at Damron Publishing since 1990. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 31 ======= 31 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 4084327
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Brenda MarstonAndrea Hektor
Date completed:
August 2003
EAD encoding:
Andrea HektorPeter Martinez, Nov. 2003
Date modified:
Ben Wrubel, June 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Michael Shively's personal papers, including files from his work as Associate Director of the Center for Homosexual, Education, Evaluation, and Research (CHEER) at San Francisco State University, 1975-1980; his work for Liberation Publications Inc., which published the ADVOCATE, 1980-90; and his work at Damron Publishing since 1990.
RELATED MATERIALS
Collecting Program: Human Sexuality Collection.
SERIES LIST
Series I. CHEER Boxes 1,17
Series II. Advocate/LPI Boxes 2-6
Series III. Chronological Files/Correspondence Boxes 7-15, 21-23
Series IV. Damron Boxes 16, 18-21, 24-27, 29, 30
Series V. Ephemera
Cite As:
Michael G. Shively papers, #7671. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Also goes by name Mikal Shively.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Box 7, folder 22 restricted until January 2051 or Pat Califia's death.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Box 17 restricted until 2025 or John DeCecco's death.
Gays -- Travel -- Guidebooks. Gay accommodations -- Directories. Homosexuality -- United States -- Periodicals. Gay liberation movement -- United States. Gays. Advocate (Los Angeles, Calif.) Damron Company

Scope and Contents
